prerequisites: A Linkrunner account with access to the Linkrunner dashboard.
A Data APIs server key generated from the dashboard (used as the linkrunner-key secret).
For attribution queries, know the channel or filter values you want to pull, plus the timezone and start_timestamp/end_timestamp window if you need to bound results.

authMethods.name: API key (linkrunner-key header)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate every Data API request with the linkrunner-key HTTP header set to your Linkrunner server key. This single key is the connector's only credential, mapped to the required linkrunner-key secret field. Treat it as a secret and use it server-side only.

setupSteps.title: Generate your Data APIs server key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Linkrunner dashboard and open the Data APIs settings page at https://dashboard.linkrunner.io/settings?s=data-apis. Copy the displayed server key and keep it confidential.

setupSteps.title: Provide the key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied server key as the required linkrunner-key field. The connector sends it as the linkrunner-key header on each request to the Linkrunner Data APIs.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional query scope
setupSteps.body: Optionally set channel (ad network or affiliate, for example GOOGLE, META, TIKTOK, APPLE_SEARCH_ADS) and filter (status such as ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ALL) to narrow campaigns. Set start_timestamp, end_timestamp, and timezone (an IANA timezone identifier such as America/New_York) to bound and interpret the time window. All of these are optional; leave them unset to use the API defaults.

sources.title: Data APIs - Linkrunner (authentication, server key, query parameters)
